claim_chain: Meningeal macrophages are essential for brain homeostasis but their Ca2+ dynamics are unknown
We developed intravital imaging to characterize macrophage Ca2+ activity at steady state
Macrophages in different niches exhibit distinct Ca2+ signal properties
Dural perivascular macrophage Ca2+ activity is coupled to vasomotion
CSD triggers opposing Ca2+ responses in different macrophage subsets
CGRP receptor signaling mediates persistent Ca2+ elevations post-CSD
These findings reveal biological diversity and neuroimmune signaling complexity

typical_pattern: Opening statement of what is known or unknown
Description of what the study demonstrates or reveals
Acknowledgment of limitations or need for further study

likely_priorities: Specificity of cell labeling and potential contamination from other cell types
Temporal resolution and potential missed fast Ca2+ transients
Generalizability beyond extrasinusoidal macrophages to sinus-associated cells
Direct versus indirect effects of pharmacological manipulations
Functional consequences of observed Ca2+ dynamics
likely_attacks: PF4-Cre labeling may include non-macrophage cells with low-level PF4 expression
Downsampling to 1 Hz could miss fast Ca2+ transients or microdomain activity
Extrasinusoidal macrophages may not represent all dural macrophage populations
CGRP receptor antagonism does not prove macrophage-specific signaling
Lack of causal manipulation to test macrophage-vascular coupling
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The limitations paragraph explicitly states: (1) PF4-Cre labeling cannot fully exclude expression in a small subset of meningeal dendritic cells, monocytes, and T cells; (2) downsampling to ~1 Hz could have missed fast Ca2+ transients or microdomain activity; (3) imaging was restricted to extrasinusoidal macrophages which may exhibit distinct properties from sinus-associated cells; (4) pharmacological approach does not establish macrophage-specific CGRP signaling. The discussion also notes that studying whether macrophage Ca2+ signaling regulates dural vasomotion will require an experimental approach that has yet to be developed.

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, invented numbers, or author names were included. Claim strength and hedge examples are directly quoted. Reviewer concerns are inferred exclusively from the paper's own limitations section.
